citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) from the Attending Veterinarian (AV) states that juvenile cats will be
vaccinated for Rabies at 12 weeks of age. During the review of the animal records, it was noted that the following five
juvenile cats have not been vaccinated for rabies: #653 Peppermint, calico, DOB 8/26/25 (31 weeks of age as of 3/31/26);
#649 Charlie, brown tabby, DOB 8/18/25 (32 weeks of age as of 3/31/26); #652 Tinsel, black/white, DOB 8/26/25 (31
weeks of age as of 3/31/26); #656 Birch, black/white, DOB 8/26/25 (31 weeks of age as of 3/31/26); #654 Snowlyn, tortie,
DOB 8/26/25 (31 weeks of age as of 3/31/26).
Per this Section, each exhibitor shall maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that includes the use of appropriate
methods to prevent and control diseases. The written PVC contains guidance on the appropriate methods to prevent and
control diseases that are recommended by the AV. The applicant needs to follow the AV’s vaccination schedule guidance
in the written PVC for these five cats.

The following items were identified that were not compliant with the guidance from the Attending Veterinarian (AV) in the
written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) for Dogs.
(1) The written PVC states that the initial vaccination for rabies is administered at 12 weeks of age. During the review of
the animal records, it was noted that the following three Shih Tzu dogs born on 10/5/25 (25 weeks of age as of 3/31/26)
have not been vaccinated for rabies: #675 Lou, white; #675 Fred, tan/white; and #677 Donna, tan/white.
(2) The written PVC states that adult dogs are to be vaccinated for distemper, parvo, and hepatitis annually (DHP). During
the review of the animal records, it was noted that the following three adult Shih Tzu dogs have not been vaccinated as
per the schedule in the written PVC: #462 Lily, brown/white, DOB 10/10/20, last DHP vaccination was 2/28/25; #471
Zoey, brown/white, DOB 12/7/21, last DHP vaccination was 2/28/25; and Cindy Lou, tan/white, DOB 10/8/18, last DHP
vaccination was 2/28/25.
(3) The written PVC does not include specific information regarding the sampling and treatment of parasites. Under the
Parasite Control section for Ectoparasites and Blood Parasites, it only says “monthly” and under the Intestinal Parasites, it
only says “every 6 months”.
Per this Section, each exhibitor must follow the Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) as developed, written, and signed by
the Attending Veterinarian (AV). The written PVC must address the vaccinations for contagious and/or deadly diseases of
dogs (including rabies, parvovirus and distemper) and sampling/treatment of parasites and other pests (including fleas,
worms, coccidia, giardia, and heartworm) in accordance with a schedule approved by the AV. The applicant needs to
follow the AV’s vaccination schedule guidance in the written PVC for these six dogs, and the written PVC needs to include
specific information regarding sampling and treatment of parasites.
This is a 1st Re-license Inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
Applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection(s) or by 5/31/26 (60 days) or the applicant will forfeit the license
fee and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Relicense Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires on 4/27/26.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) from the Attending Veterinarian (AV) states that juvenile cats will be 
vaccinated for Rabies at 12 weeks of age. During the review of the animal records, it was noted that the following five 
juvenile cats have not been vaccinated for rabies: #653 Peppermint, calico, DOB 8/26/25 (31 weeks of age as of 3/31/26); 
#649 Charlie, brown tabby, DOB 8/18/25 (32 weeks of age as of 3/31/26); #652 Tinsel, black/white, DOB 8/26/25 (31 
weeks of age as of 3/31/26); #656 Birch, black/white, DOB 8/26/25 (31 weeks of age as of 3/31/26); #654 Snowlyn, tortie, 
DOB 8/26/25 (31 weeks of age as of 3/31/26). 
 
Per this Section, each exhibitor shall maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that includes the use of appropriate 
methods to prevent and control diseases. The written PVC contains guidance on the appropriate methods to prevent and 
control diseases that are recommended by the AV. The applicant needs to follow the AV’s vaccination schedule guidance 
in the written PVC for these five cats. 
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The following items were identified that were not compliant with the guidance from the Attending Veterinarian (AV) in the 
written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) for Dogs. 
 
(1) The written PVC states that the initial vaccination for rabies is administered at 12 weeks of age. During the review of 
the animal records, it was noted that the following three Shih Tzu dogs born on 10/5/25 (25 weeks of age as of 3/31/26) 
have not been vaccinated for rabies: #675 Lou, white; #675 Fred, tan/white; and #677 Donna, tan/white.  
(2) The written PVC states that adult dogs are to be vaccinated for distemper, parvo, and hepatitis annually (DHP). During 
the review of the animal records, it was noted that the following three adult Shih Tzu dogs have not been vaccinated as 
per the schedule in the written PVC: #462 Lily, brown/white, DOB 10/10/20, last DHP vaccination was 2/28/25; #471 
Zoey, brown/white, DOB 12/7/21, last DHP vaccination was 2/28/25; and Cindy Lou, tan/white, DOB 10/8/18, last DHP 
vaccination was 2/28/25.  
(3) The written PVC does not include specific information regarding the sampling and treatment of parasites. Under the 
Parasite Control section for Ectoparasites and Blood Parasites, it only says “monthly” and under the Intestinal Parasites, it 
only says “every 6 months”.

Per this Section, each exhibitor must follow the Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) as developed, written, and signed by 
the Attending Veterinarian (AV). The written PVC must address the vaccinations for contagious and/or deadly diseases of 
dogs (including rabies, parvovirus and distemper) and sampling/treatment of parasites and other pests (including fleas, 
worms, coccidia, giardia, and heartworm) in accordance with a schedule approved by the AV. The applicant needs to 
follow the AV’s vaccination schedule guidance in the written PVC for these six dogs, and the written PVC needs to include 
specific information regarding sampling and treatment of parasites. 
 
This is a 1st Re-license Inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
Applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection(s) or by 5/31/26 (60 days) or the applicant will forfeit the license 
fee and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Relicense Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires on 4/27/26.

species.common: DOMESTIC PIG / POTBELLY PIG / MICRO PIG
